OB_CON_001.title: Defines supervised-learning vocabulary accurately
OB_CON_001.description: Learner correctly recalls or explains core supervised-learning terms such as object, feature, label, dataset, class, or instance in a decision-tree context.
required_evidence: Correct use of at least one foundational term in learner-produced text or speech
Term usage is domain-appropriate, not merely copied prompt wording
possible_misconceptions: Confuses feature with label or object with class
Uses terminology without demonstrating understanding of meaning
OB_CON_001.leakage_guard: Eloquent phrasing without correct term meaning must not inflate strength (CLT-A).

OB_CON_002.title: Distinguishes related decision-tree concepts
OB_CON_002.description: Learner differentiates closely related concepts such as rule versus tree, threshold versus split, or training versus test purpose.
required_evidence: Explicit contrast or correct independent use of two related concepts
Distinction is learner-generated, not restated task instructions
possible_misconceptions: Treats all tree components as interchangeable labels
Equates any numeric cutoff with a meaningful threshold
OB_CON_002.leakage_guard: Terminology recall without distinction supports OB_CON_001 only, not broader understanding.

OB_CON_003.title: Explains purpose and mechanism of decision-tree classification
OB_CON_003.description: Learner explains what a decision tree does in classification, including how features and thresholds produce class decisions.
required_evidence: Learner links features, thresholds, or rules to class outcomes
Explanation references decision-tree logic, not generic problem solving
possible_misconceptions: Describes trees as memorization without rule structure
Attributes classification to intuition rather than explicit criteria
OB_CON_003.leakage_guard: Generic AI enthusiasm without DT mechanism is not admissible.

OB_CON_004.title: Explains role of training data in model learning
OB_CON_004.description: Learner explains why labeled data is needed for supervised learning and how models learn patterns from examples.
required_evidence: Learner connects data examples to model learning or parameter estimation
Explanation distinguishes learning from mere rule lookup
possible_misconceptions: Believes models learn without data or labels
Confuses training data with test evaluation data
OB_CON_004.leakage_guard: Broad data-literacy statements without DT linkage are insufficient.

OB_CON_005.title: States or explains evaluation metric definitions
OB_CON_005.description: Learner states or explains metrics such as misclassification rate, sensitivity, specificity, or confusion-matrix cell meanings.
required_evidence: Correct metric definition or formula in learner text
Metric named is tied to classification performance, not unrelated statistics
possible_misconceptions: Swaps sensitivity and specificity
Treats accuracy as sufficient when class imbalance matters
OB_CON_005.leakage_guard: Formula recall without application supports conceptual dimension only.

OB_CON_006.title: Interprets relationships among confusion-matrix components
OB_CON_006.description: Learner interprets how true positives, false positives, false negatives, and true negatives relate to one another in model evaluation.
required_evidence: Learner references at least two confusion-matrix cells or outcomes coherently
Interpretation explains error type, not isolated numeric recall
possible_misconceptions: Focuses on one cell while ignoring trade-offs
Mislabels error types when describing outcomes
OB_CON_006.leakage_guard: Numeric manipulation without interpretive linkage is procedural at best.

OB_PRO_001.title: Applies threshold to classify a case
OB_PRO_001.description: Learner uses a stated or selected threshold to assign a class label to an instance or group.
required_evidence: Documented classification outcome tied to a threshold value
Threshold application is learner-driven, not system-default only
possible_misconceptions: Applies threshold inconsistently across similar cases
Classifies without referencing the decision criterion
OB_PRO_001.leakage_guard: Brief responses with correct classification still admissible (CLT-B).

OB_PRO_002.title: Follows decision path to classify
OB_PRO_002.description: Learner traverses or applies a sequence of feature tests along a tree path to reach a class outcome.
required_evidence: Ordered feature-threshold decisions leading to a class assignment
Path logic is traceable in learner work or action log
possible_misconceptions: Skips intermediate nodes in multi-level trees
Assigns class at branch inconsistent with path conditions
OB_PRO_002.leakage_guard: Single-step threshold tasks may not require this behaviour.

OB_PRO_003.title: Constructs or extends decision-tree structure
OB_PRO_003.description: Learner builds or extends a tree representation with nodes, splits, and leaves that form a coherent classification structure.
required_evidence: Partial or complete tree structure authored or manipulated by learner
Structure includes at least one split and resulting branches
possible_misconceptions: Creates structurally invalid trees with missing branches
Duplicates nodes without classification purpose
OB_PRO_003.leakage_guard: Tool-default tree refresh without learner decisions is insufficient.

OB_PRO_004.title: Orders decision-tree workflow steps correctly
OB_PRO_004.description: Learner sequences procedural steps such as feature selection, dimension arrangement, threshold finding, and classification in a valid order.
required_evidence: Learner-produced ordering of at least three workflow steps
Order respects dependencies between selection, thresholding, and decision
possible_misconceptions: Places threshold selection before feature identification
Omits evaluation or decision step at end of workflow
OB_PRO_004.leakage_guard: Ordering without execution supports procedural workflow only, not strategic mastery.

OB_PRO_005.title: Articulates explicit if-then decision rule
OB_PRO_005.description: Learner states a decision rule linking feature conditions to class outcomes in natural or symbolic language.
required_evidence: If-then or equivalent conditional rule in learner text
Rule references at least one feature and one class outcome
possible_misconceptions: States class label without conditional antecedent
Rule contradicts demonstrated classification behaviour
OB_PRO_005.leakage_guard: Fluent rule wording with incorrect execution triggers PAT-CONTRA-001.

OB_PRO_006.title: Assigns class labels at tree nodes
OB_PRO_006.description: Learner assigns class labels to root, inner, or leaf nodes consistent with a tree structure under construction.
required_evidence: Documented class label at a named tree position
Label assignment is learner-authored in construction task
possible_misconceptions: Assigns conflicting labels on same branch path
Leaves internal nodes without resolution when required
OB_PRO_006.leakage_guard: Auto-filled tool labels without learner action are not admissible.

OB_PRO_007.title: Sets split thresholds at root or inner nodes
OB_PRO_007.description: Learner selects or records threshold or split values at root or inner nodes during tree construction or tuning.
required_evidence: Threshold or split value set at a named node position
Value is learner-selected or learner-recorded, not read-only display
possible_misconceptions: Uses arbitrary cutoff without reference to data or task
Changes threshold without updating downstream classifications
OB_PRO_007.leakage_guard: High click volume without interpretable threshold values is insufficient (CLT inverse of L3).

OB_PRO_008.title: Manipulates exploration tool to change splits or thresholds
OB_PRO_008.description: Learner performs meaningful tool actions that change split values, tree structure, or model refresh in an interactive environment.
required_evidence: Logged or observed change_split_values, refresh_tree, or equivalent meaningful action
Action sequence is interpretable as deliberate exploration, not UI noise
possible_misconceptions: Repeated random dragging without threshold change
Session events mistaken for pedagogical manipulation
OB_PRO_008.leakage_guard: Digital fluency alone cannot support strategic claims; ceiling is moderate without justification.

OB_PRO_009.title: Computes metric values from given data
OB_PRO_009.description: Learner calculates or derives metric values such as MCR or sensitivity from provided counts or tables.
required_evidence: Numeric result with traceable calculation steps or correct final value
Computation uses confusion-matrix or performance inputs
possible_misconceptions: Arithmetic error with correct formula recall
Uses wrong denominator for rate metrics
OB_PRO_009.leakage_guard: Computation without interpretation does not support strategic evaluation.

OB_STR_001.title: Compares alternative thresholds using performance evidence
OB_STR_001.description: Learner compares two or more threshold or model options using stated performance indicators such as error rates or separation.
required_evidence: Explicit comparison between at least two alternatives
Comparison cites performance evidence, not preference alone
possible_misconceptions: Compares thresholds without referencing outcomes
Selects lower numeric value without performance rationale
OB_STR_001.leakage_guard: Narrative comparison without performance linkage is inadmissible for strategic dimension.

OB_STR_002.title: Selects threshold or feature using explicit criteria
OB_STR_002.description: Learner chooses a threshold, split feature, or model parameter and states the criterion used for selection.
required_evidence: Documented selection decision with named criterion
Criterion links to task goal or performance measure
possible_misconceptions: Selects first available feature without evaluation
Uses popularity or intuition as sole criterion
OB_STR_002.leakage_guard: Correct selection without stated criterion supports procedural execution at most.

OB_STR_003.title: Justifies model or threshold choice with evidence
OB_STR_003.description: Learner provides evidence-based rationale for a model structure, threshold, or classification decision.
required_evidence: Rationale references observable performance or data pattern
Justification is learner-authored and specific to the decision made
possible_misconceptions: Post-hoc justification disconnected from actual choice
Confuses plausibility with evidence
OB_STR_003.leakage_guard: Polished writing without performance linkage must not inflate strategic strength (CLT-A).

OB_STR_004.title: Identifies predictive features from observed data patterns
OB_STR_004.description: Learner names a feature and cites an observed data or graph pattern suggesting predictive value for classification.
required_evidence: Named feature plus described pattern such as separation or distribution difference
Pattern reference is observational, not guesswork alone
possible_misconceptions: Names feature without citing observed evidence
Cites graph trend unrelated to class distinction
OB_STR_004.leakage_guard: Prior belief without pattern citation is OB_REF_004, not this behaviour.

OB_STR_005.title: Evaluates trade-offs between error types
OB_STR_005.description: Learner discusses or applies reasoning about false positives versus false negatives or sensitivity versus misclassification trade-offs.
required_evidence: Explicit trade-off reasoning between two error types or metrics
Reasoning connects to threshold or model decision context
possible_misconceptions: Optimizes one metric while ignoring complementary error
Treats all errors as equally costly without task context
OB_STR_005.leakage_guard: Vocabulary mention of FP/FN without trade-off reasoning is conceptual only.

OB_STR_006.title: Iteratively explores parameter combinations affecting performance
OB_STR_006.description: Learner systematically tests multiple parameter or threshold combinations and observes resulting performance changes.
required_evidence: Evidence of multiple tested combinations with performance outcomes
Iteration is deliberate, not single-trial
possible_misconceptions: Repeats same parameter without variation
Changes parameters without recording outcomes
OB_STR_006.leakage_guard: Exploration volume without outcome interpretation caps at moderate.

OB_STR_007.title: Synthesizes exploration into justified parameter choice
OB_STR_007.description: Learner integrates results from iterative exploration into a final justified threshold or parameter selection.
required_evidence: Final selection linked to prior exploration results
Synthesis statement references compared outcomes
possible_misconceptions: Final choice contradicts documented exploration
Synthesis narrative without supporting trials
OB_STR_007.leakage_guard: Requires traceable link between exploration and final choice.

OB_STR_008.title: Optimizes threshold performance across conditions
OB_STR_008.description: Learner identifies best-performing threshold or configuration across multiple rows, cases, or experimental conditions in tabular or tool context.
required_evidence: Selection of optimal or best-performing option from multiple candidates
Performance criterion is explicit in learner work
possible_misconceptions: Picks maximum value without performance column reference
Confuses local optimum with global best across table
OB_STR_008.leakage_guard: Table lookup without criterion articulation supports procedural accuracy at most.

OB_STR_009.title: Interprets model feedback in applied decision context
OB_STR_009.description: Learner interprets model output or feedback and relates it to a practical classification or teaching decision.
required_evidence: Interpretation of model output tied to actionable decision
Feedback element is referenced specifically, not generically
possible_misconceptions: Restates feedback without interpretation
Draws decision unrelated to displayed metrics
OB_STR_009.leakage_guard: Generic model praise without metric reference is inadmissible.

OB_REF_001.title: Reflects on learning or misconceptions about decision trees
OB_REF_001.description: Learner articulates what was learned, misunderstood, or found surprising about decision-tree concepts or tasks.
required_evidence: Metacognitive statement about own understanding or confusion
Content is specific to decision-tree learning, not generic study habits
possible_misconceptions: Generic reflection without DT content
Claims mastery without evidential reference
OB_REF_001.leakage_guard: Excellent reflection without correct execution does not support procedural or strategic claims (CLT-D).

OB_REF_002.title: Articulates model limitations or uncertainty
OB_REF_002.description: Learner discusses limits of a model, data, or threshold choice including uncertainty, imperfect classification, or overfitting risk.
required_evidence: Statement of limitation or uncertainty tied to model or data
Limitation is substantive, not token disclaimer
possible_misconceptions: Claims perfect classification when evidence shows errors
Confuses model uncertainty with personal hesitation only
OB_REF_002.leakage_guard: Limitation discourse without tie to observed model behaviour is weak reflective only.

OB_REF_003.title: Revises belief after new evidence
OB_REF_003.description: Learner changes or updates a prior statement in light of new data, tool exploration, or feedback.
required_evidence: Documented prior and posterior positions or explicit change statement
Revision is linked to cited new evidence
possible_misconceptions: Changes answer without acknowledging prior position
Revision follows peer copying rather than evidence
OB_REF_003.leakage_guard: Requires temporal or cross-item traceability between prior and revised claims.

OB_REF_004.title: States uninformed prior before exploration
OB_REF_004.description: Learner records initial belief or prediction about features, classes, or model behaviour before structured data exploration.
required_evidence: Prior belief stated before exposure to analytic evidence in task design
Belief concerns predictive feature, class tendency, or model expectation
possible_misconceptions: Post-hoc answer presented as prior belief
Prior copied from instructional example
OB_REF_004.leakage_guard: Baseline diagnostic only; excluded from mastery aggregation per Inference Rules R16.
